rulings.Case Title: SAMSARA INC. VS. MOTIVE TECHNOLOGIES, INC,
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 4, 2026, Line 1, RESPONDENT MOTIVE TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. The unopposed motion for pro hac vice admission of C. Bryan Wilson is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL VS. AMAZON.COM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 4, 2026, Line 2, PLAINTIFF KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL's Motion For Approval Of Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. The court finds that the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code, sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law on the evidence before the court; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code, section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. The parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. KENNETH RUNYON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One From Defendant Ena Rosario Reales
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 4, 2026, Line 3, PLAINTIFF SARAH LEWIS' Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One From Defendant Ena Rosario Reales. Plaintiff Sarah Lewis's unopposed motion to compel responses to requests for production of documents from defendant Ena Rosario Reales is denied. Lewis provides a declaration that describes the dispute and acknowledges that Reales made service of purported responses. But she does not provide the responses or copies of her meet and confer efforts. A party's assurance to the court that it has received inadequate responses is not a sufficient basis to grant relief. Moreover, because Lewis received purported responses, this is a motion to compel further responses and must be accompanied by a separate statement, which Lewis does not provide. (Rule of Court 3.1345.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH BLOCK VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 4, 2026, Line 4, 1- DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued on the court's own motion to May 18, 2026. Plaintiff filed his Second Amended Complaint in federal court but the complaint is not on file as a pleading in this court. The court grants leave to plaintiff to file the Second Amended Complaint attached as Exhibit B to the Shoyket Declaration, but with a state-court caption, by May 11, 2026. Defendant's demurrer to that pleading shall be deemed filed on May 12, 2026, and the court will decide the demurrer on the current briefing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JESSICA BLACK VS. GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE HIGHWAY TRANSPORTATION DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Site Inspection Of Defendant'S Premises
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion and Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 4, 2026, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JESSICA BLACK's Motion To Compel Site Inspection Of Defendant's Premises. Plaintiff Jessica Black's motion to compel site inspection of defendant's premises, and for sanctions, is granted in part, denied in part, and continued for further hearing to May 27, 2026.
